name: Restore The Compact Two-Stage PTX Anchor After The Failed Wait-Sync-Collapse Variant

summary.name: Restore The Compact Two-Stage PTX Anchor After The Failed Wait-Sync-Collapse Variant
summary.hypothesis: The latest wait-sync-collapse variant regressed from the restored compact anchor, so the next round should first return the code to the last known clean compact two-stage PTX seam before testing a different family.
summary.expected_bottleneck: The immediate problem is the regressing wait_sync_collapse variant itself, which raised barrier and registers on the active compact surface.
summary.risk: Low. This is a narrow restore to the last clean compact PTX hot loop and does not change tiling, staging depth, or shared-memory budget.
ranking_notes: The current branch is only 0.15 ms off the restored anchor, so recovering the clean compact seam is the shortest path back to an interpretable search surface.
This is the right next move because the selected family, not the anchor itself, caused the regression.
A clean restore also sets up the round-15 checkpoint on a stable measured surface instead of a known regression.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if restoring the seam requires touching any code outside the compact PTX hot loop or if the restore does not bring the loop back to the known 201-register class.
expected_local_changes: Restore the compact PTX hot loop from the refill-before-wait variant back to the last clean two-stage anchor.
Keep grouped_rows=4, the 128x128 dispatch, and the 22,016 B shared-memory footprint unchanged.
Do not combine the restore with another sync-family tweak in the same round.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
